jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening sequence couch gag presumably goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated to his character but routine for the show

jokes.text: If your mother wasn't so fancy... we could shop at the gas station like normal people.
jokes.setup: Family shopping for fancy grocery items
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards view of what constitutes 'normal' shopping
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Homer's skewed priorities and class consciousness

jokes.text: I need this candy for school. Candy class.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to justify buying candy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake class name delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and absurdist logic typical of Bart's schemes

jokes.text: But get five bags in case we eat four on the way home.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to buy candy for Bart
jokes.punchline: Revealing his own lack of self-control with specific math
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's self-awareness of his own weakness

jokes.text: My teacher said I need cupcakes- cupcakes to learn.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to justify buying treats like her brother
jokes.punchline: Smart Lisa's awkward attempt at Bart-style manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Lisa trying and failing to be as deceptive as Bart

jokes.text: You know, I always felt sorry for Marge, having to do the shopping... but this is kind of fun.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering shopping while Marge is absent
jokes.punchline: His obliviousness to why Marge might not enjoy it as much
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Homer's self-centered perspective

jokes.text: Yeah! I wish Maggie had to go to the emergency room more often.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying his shopping experience
jokes.punchline: Wishing harm on his baby for personal convenience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly suited to Homer's selfishness, shocking but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hmm. I never heard of a baby swallowing a magazine before- And I'm a doctor.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert examining baby who swallowed magazine
jokes.punchline: Professional credentials used to emphasize how unusual this is
jokes.explanation: Mild professional humor, sets up for the Time magazine gag

jokes.text: I don't know if it matters, but it was a Time magazine.
jokes.setup: Homer providing additional detail about what Maggie swallowed
jokes.punchline: Sets up the 'Time'/'time' wordplay that follows
jokes.explanation: Setup for the following punchline, functional but not inherently funny

jokes.text: Does that matter? - Uh-oh. This could be dangerous. - What? What? Syrian hard-liners are gaining influence.
jokes.setup: Medical concern about what baby swallowed
jokes.punchline: Doctor concerned about magazine content, not medical danger
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - builds medical tension then deflates with political content concern

jokes.text: I've already bagged it by color and in order of each item's discovery by man.
jokes.setup: Customer wants groceries re-bagged in specific order
jokes.punchline: Bag boy's overly elaborate organizational system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - mundane job elevated to anthropological science

jokes.text: The customer's always right. That's what everybody likes about us. Now, mush!
jokes.setup: Customer invoking customer service principle
jokes.punchline: Immediately being rude and contradicting the principle
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor about customer entitlement and self-awareness

jokes.text: You tell him, Jumbo. And you- Start over. I want everything in one bag. But I don't want the bag to be heavy.
jokes.setup: Customer making impossible demands about bagging
jokes.punchline: Contradictory requirements that can't be fulfilled
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of customer absurdity, logical impossibility played for laughs

jokes.text: What are you, the possible police? Just do it.
jokes.setup: Bag boy suggesting something isn't possible
jokes.punchline: Customer's nonsensical authority figure reference
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay with 'possible police' - absurd but memorable phrase

jokes.text: Hey, hey, watch what you're doin' there, sack monkey. You're bruising my Dura-Log.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about grocery handling
jokes.punchline: Dehumanizing term and concern for fake firelog
jokes.explanation: Good satire of customer entitlement, 'sack monkey' is memorably dehumanizing

jokes.text: Hurry up! I can't stand here jabbing you all day.
jokes.setup: Impatient customer complaining
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of physical abuse as time constraint
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of customer abuse, treating violence as mere inconvenience

jokes.text: Bag boys have feelings too, you know. - No, you don't.
jokes.setup: Bag boy asserting his humanity
jokes.punchline: Customer's matter-of-fact denial of his humanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of dehumanization, economical and brutal

jokes.text: On behalf of Sack Stuffers local 199... I'm callin' a strike.
jokes.setup: Workplace conflict escalating
jokes.punchline: Formal union organization for lowly bag boys with official local number
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - applying serious labor organization to menial job

jokes.text: Oh, the bag boys are on strike. Ooh! I'm shakin'.
jokes.setup: Bag boy strike being announced
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive sarcasm about the strike's importance
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response, fits Homer's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: If I can have this rubber stick.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to leave the store
jokes.punchline: Bizarre condition about taking random object
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's random fixations and childlike behavior

jokes.text: Pledging to honor the bag boys' strike... are the Brotherhood of Fruit Packers and Unpackers... the Shelf-Dusters' Union... and the Unattractive Waitresses of America.
jokes.setup: Other unions supporting the bag boy strike
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous union names, ending with the brutal honesty of 'Unattractive Waitresses'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with great final punchline - unexpected honesty in union naming

jokes.text: Kiss my grits!
jokes.setup: Unattractive waitress being interviewed
jokes.punchline: Reference to Flo from Alice TV show
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference, fits the waitress character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No bag boys are gonna stop Lenny... from hosting a casual get-together. - Casual? I can taste that get-together now.
jokes.setup: Lenny claiming the strike won't stop his party
jokes.punchline: Carl's reaction suggesting it's not actually casual
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment about Lenny's party pretensions, but not strongly executed

jokes.text: SulfurJerky? Cream of Toast? Where do we get all this crap?
jokes.setup: Family desperate for food, looking through pantry
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake food product names
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist product names, but fairly standard Simpsons fake brand humor

jokes.text: Most of it was sent by relatives who couldn't see very well.
jokes.setup: Question about where terrible food came from
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation about visually impaired gift-givers
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery explaining absurd situation with mundane logic

jokes.text: Oh, that Amos Burke made his own rules.
jokes.setup: Homer finding his old lunch box with TV character on it
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reference to obscure 1960s TV detective
jokes.explanation: Obscure cultural reference that fits Homer's age, but limited appeal

jokes.text: Very old animal crackers! Mmm. Turbulent.
jokes.setup: Homer eating extremely old crackers
jokes.punchline: Describing 1960s taste as 'turbulent' (the era, not the crackers)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'turbulent' works both as flavor description and historical period reference

jokes.text: Find the golden giraffe, and we'll send you and your family to Africa.
jokes.setup: Homer finding contest prize in old crackers
jokes.punchline: Reading the contest rules that drive the episode plot
jokes.explanation: Plot setup more than joke, but the absurdity of the old contest is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Africa? They're bound to have food there.
jokes.setup: Homer learning about Africa trip
jokes.punchline: His naive assumption about food availability
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's geographic ignorance and food obsession

jokes.text: I want to shoot a lion in the face, fight Muhammad Ali... and ride in a convertible with two happy zebras.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his African vacation expectations
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and inappropriate activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity mixing violence, boxing legends, and cartoon imagery

jokes.text: We don't even make animal crackers anymore. We make household poisons and Christmas lights.
jokes.setup: Company explaining they can't honor the contest
jokes.punchline: Bizarre product line shift to dangerous items
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate absurdity - innocent snacks to household hazards is perfect escalation

jokes.text: Your box made promises of a 'vacational' nature... and I expect you to live up to them.
jokes.setup: Homer making legal argument about the contest
jokes.punchline: Using made-up word 'vacational' while trying to sound official
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Homer trying to sound sophisticated but mangling language

jokes.text: Sir, with all due respect, an old box of cookies is not a legal contract.
jokes.setup: Legal professional responding to Homer's demand
jokes.punchline: Obvious legal reality stated formally
jokes.explanation: Straight man response, functional but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Ow! My eye! - Is that our fault? - We're in big, big trouble.
jokes.setup: Homer getting hurt by the box
jokes.punchline: Company's immediate panic about liability
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick escalation showing corporate fear of lawsuits

jokes.text: On the plane I'm gonna need two seats- for the twins.
jokes.setup: Homer making seating arrangements
jokes.punchline: Referring to his fat rolls as 'twins'
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about Homer's weight, creative euphemism delivery

jokes.text: Evil is coming. - What shall we do, N'gungo? - You are N'gungo now. Aaah!
jokes.setup: African shaman predicting doom
jokes.punchline: Passing leadership responsibility to someone unprepared
jokes.explanation: Good escalation gag about unwanted leadership responsibility

jokes.text: Attention, passengers. Please prepare for our landing in Tanzania. I'm sorry. It is now called 'New Zanzibar.' Excuse me. It is now called 'Pepsi Presents New Zanzibar.'
jokes.setup: Pilot announcing destination
jokes.punchline: Rapid political changes culminating in corporate sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating political satire ending with perfect corporate sponsorship punchline

jokes.text: Isn't that cute? A bush baby. - Where? - Aaah! Shoo! Shoo!
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out cute African wildlife
jokes.punchline: The 'cute' animal attacking her aggressively
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection gag - expectation of cuteness vs. reality of wild animals

jokes.text: Who's Muntu? - He is our leader. He seized power in a bloodless coup- all smotherings.
jokes.setup: Tourist asking about local leader
jokes.punchline: Contradiction in 'bloodless' coup using smothering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay - technically bloodless but still violent, perfect logical contradiction

jokes.text: Just like Jimmy Carter.
jokes.setup: Description of violent political coup
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre comparison to peaceful ex-president
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comparison - violent African dictator to peaceful humanitarian president

jokes.text: When do we get to see the animals? In the morning, little one. Now, good night, and don't let the bedbugs paralyze.
jokes.setup: Traditional bedtime saying about bedbugs
jokes.punchline: Escalating 'bite' to 'paralyze' for African context
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of familiar saying to reflect more dangerous environment

jokes.text: All right! I got another one. - Hey, you didn't see a warthog. I'm looking at one right now.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about spotting wildlife
jokes.punchline: Bart insulting Homer by calling him a warthog
jokes.explanation: Standard family insult humor, fits character dynamics but predictable

jokes.text: Wow! It just rolled over to 10,000.
jokes.setup: Family watching majestic elephants
jokes.punchline: Homer focused on odometer instead of wildlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects nature appreciation, gets Homer's mundane car obsession

jokes.text: Wait. Rhinos don't come from eggs. - What did you just see, Lisa? - I know, but- - What did you just see?
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning impossible wildlife observation
jokes.punchline: Guide forcing her to accept nonsensical reality
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about Lisa's logic conflicting with tour guide authority

jokes.text: Look, Mother, by that tree- Cheetah. - Mmm. He doesn't look so fast to me.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out famous fast animal
jokes.punchline: Homer's unimpressed assessment based on visual appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's literal-mindedness about animal reputations

jokes.text: Glibby glup gloopy, Nibby nobby nooby, La lee lo, lo, lo
jokes.setup: African guide singing traditional song
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical attempt to join in with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Character humor about cultural insensitivity and Homer's confident ignorance

jokes.text: Now, honey, poachers are nature's way of keeping the balance. Whenever there are so many species that people get confused and angry... a poacher is born.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing outrage about poaching
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely backwards environmental philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of environmental ignorance - completely inverted logic delivered with confidence

jokes.text: I've got more bones than that guy. If you're trying to impress me, you've failed.
jokes.setup: Guide showing important 2-million-year-old fossil
jokes.punchline: Homer's unimpressed competitive response about bone quantity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the point of archaeological significance entirely

jokes.text: By the way, what kind of blood is this? - Cow. - [All Gasp, Laugh]
jokes.setup: Family concerned about blood they're drinking
jokes.punchline: Relief that it's 'only' cow blood
jokes.explanation: Good cultural shock humor - relieved reaction to still-disgusting reality

jokes.text: Bart! I told you not to get your lip 'disked.'
jokes.setup: Bart getting tribal body modification
jokes.punchline: Marge's mispronunciation of 'pierced' as 'disked'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's malapropism while trying to be culturally aware

jokes.text: This song has been going on for hours. Yeah. It's like the Allman Brothers.
jokes.setup: Traditional African drumming continuing
jokes.punchline: Comparison to notoriously long rock songs
jokes.explanation: Good cultural comparison humor connecting African traditions to American rock

jokes.text: A hungry, hungry hippo. Aaah!
jokes.setup: Dangerous hippo approaching
jokes.punchline: Reference to children's board game
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference that fits the situation but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Quick! Into the river. Hippos hate water. - No, they don't. They-Aaah!
jokes.setup: Homer giving survival advice
jokes.punchline: Lisa correcting his obviously wrong information before being cut off by danger
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Homer's confident ignorance vs. Lisa's knowledge

jokes.text: The left! Go to the left!
jokes.setup: Family at dangerous waterfall choosing direction
jokes.punchline: Homer's indecisive hemming and hawing before decisive but potentially wrong choice
jokes.explanation: Standard comedy about indecision under pressure, but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Being eaten by a crocodile... is just like going to sleep... in a giant blender.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort family about impending death
jokes.punchline: Horrifying comparison that makes death sound worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - attempting comfort but making situation sound more terrifying

jokes.text: Now, according to animal crackers, there's no river here.
jokes.setup: Family lost and needing directions
jokes.punchline: Homer using snack food packaging as navigation tool
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating marketing imagery as accurate geographical information

jokes.text: Oh. You think they settled that bag boy strike yet?
jokes.setup: Family in mortal danger in African wilderness
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern about mundane Springfield labor dispute
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer's priorities - worried about grocery convenience during life-or-death situation

jokes.text: Oh, isn't that sweet? He named it after his wife. - No, Dr. Bushwell is a woman. - Well, now I've heard everything.
jokes.setup: Discussion about female scientist
jokes.punchline: Homer's exaggerated shock at woman being scientist
jokes.explanation: Character humor about Homer's outdated gender assumptions, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Yeah. Thanks for the grub. - Grubs. - Whatever.
jokes.setup: Homer thanking for food using slang
jokes.punchline: Literal grubs being served, Homer's indifferent response
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay setup and Homer's characteristic acceptance of anything edible

jokes.text: So I notice your home smells of feces. - Yes? - And not just monkey feces either.
jokes.setup: Homer making inappropriate observation about smell
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with more specific and offensive detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about Homer's social inappropriateness escalating

jokes.text: You must be the most boring woman on earth. - Possibly, but- I mean, I knew scientists wasted their lives, but geez!
jokes.setup: Dr. Bushwell describing her routine life
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest assessment of her lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cringe moment - saying exactly what everyone's thinking but shouldn't say

jokes.text: So like us.
jokes.setup: Homer praising chimps as noble creatures similar to humans
jokes.punchline: Chimps presumably behaving badly, proving the opposite
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing - statement immediately contradicted by visual evidence

jokes.text: The chimps are running a diamond mine.
jokes.setup: Discovery of Dr. Bushwell's secret
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous revelation about animal labor exploitation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - combining animal research with industrial exploitation

jokes.text: She's one of the 10 richest chimp researchers in the world.
jokes.setup: Dr. Bushwell's wealth being discovered
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific ranking in extremely narrow field
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist specificity - ranking wealth in impossibly narrow professional category

jokes.text: I haven't said anything for a while. Hmm.
jokes.setup: Extended dialogue scene without Homer input
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware comment about his absence from conversation
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about character absence from scene, self-referential but earned

jokes.text: These are just pictures of monkeys from famous movies.
jokes.setup: Lisa examining Dr. Bushwell's research
jokes.punchline: Discovery that scientific work is completely fraudulent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fraud revelation, but fairly straightforward discovery humor

jokes.text: You don't understand the crushing loneliness and greed.
jokes.setup: Dr. Bushwell confessing to her crimes
jokes.punchline: Pairing loneliness with greed as equal motivating factors
jokes.explanation: Good character moment combining sympathetic and unsympathetic motivations

jokes.text: I'll give you diamonds. Everybody wants diamonds. Diamonds will make everything all better. Diamonds! Diamonds!
jokes.setup: Dr. Bushwell being taken away
jokes.punchline: Desperate breakdown into manic diamond obsession
jokes.explanation: Standard villain breakdown escalation, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: What happened to President Muntu? - I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about dictator from earlier
jokes.punchline: Kitenge's reluctance to discuss, implying political reversal
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the reveal that follows, building suspense about political reversal

jokes.text: He got overthrown! Now he's just a stinkin' flight attendant.
jokes.setup: Mystery about former dictator's fate
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous career downgrade to service industry
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation payoff - from dictator to flight attendant is perfect absurd career trajectory
